concerns.text: FR-007 mandates a 'web-based simulator for human participants' to generate real data. Tasks T012g, T012h, and T039 are marked `[ ]` (incomplete) with no deliverable code or audit report. This leaves the primary data collection mechanism for the study non-functional, violating the core requirement to collect real data from human participants.

Constitution Principle VI mandates recruitment through 'disability advocacy organizations' and a minimum sample size of 30. There is no task in `tasks.md` to execute this recruitment process, verify the sample size, or log the recruitment channel, making the study's ethical compliance and statistical validity impossible to achieve.

T019c (validator implementation) and T019b (schema generation) are marked `[X]` (complete) in the task list, but the 'Rejected' section explicitly states the schema file `contracts/session.schema.yaml` is missing and the validator function is truncated. Marking these as complete when the artifacts are missing is a semantic weakening of the requirement to have a functional validation pipeline.

T029b and T029c (GitHub Actions reproducibility workflow) are marked `[X]`, but the 'Rejected' section confirms `github/workflows/reproducibility_check.yml` is missing. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which requires the pipeline to run on a fresh GitHub Actions runner.

T030 (deterministic notebook) is marked `[X]` but the 'Rejected' section states no seed-pinning code, exact file paths, or checksum verification artifacts exist. This directly contradicts Constitution Principle I and NFR-001 which mandate reproducibility via pinned seeds and deterministic runs.

T012g (Accessibility Accommodations) and T012h (Disability Type Selection) are marked `[X]`, but T037 (Automated Accessibility Audit) and T038 (Refinement) are marked `- [ ]`. Without the audit and refinement tasks, there is no verification that the 'Accessibility Accommodations' actually meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards as implied by the project's core goal, creating a false sense of compliance.
